title: Update cri-o annotations
body:
Hey, this PR simply updates the cri-o annotations used by libpod. My main intention would be to reuse them within cri-o and remove the code duplication there.

I'm not sure if this goes into the right direction. Code which belongs to cri-o would be maintained within libpod. A more sane alternative could be to vendor cri-o here and reuse their annotations. OTOH this would increase the dependency spider web even more, also in relation to libraries used by both, cri-o and libpod.

A third alternative could be to extract the parts used by cri-o and libpod into a package of common usage, like it has been done with containers/{storage,image}.

WDYT?

Refers to #2791

comments.body:
Hi @saschagrunert. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) or [openshift](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
@saschagrunert Thanks I want to go this way.  It is time we start vendoring more of libpod into CRI-O and we never want to go the other way.

Sure, LGTM.

We aren't really using most of these (I think cmd/podman explicitly sets most of them, but to the empty string, which is wierd), but keeping up to date is definitely desirable.
If we had some sort of common code project for containers/ projects, I'd vote to stick these annotations there, outside of CRI-O or Libpod. Doesn't seem to fit in Image or Storage, though.

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Changes LGTM. We can talk about moving this into a common repo, if desirable, later
